subject: Human Resources
title: Child Labor Laws
description: A quick, practical guide to Child Labor Laws.

hook.text: In 1900, two million American children worked in mines, mills, and factories. Some were as young as five.

buildup.text: The FLSA restricts employment of minors—limiting hours, types of work, and hazardous occupations for those under 18.

discovery.text: Children under 14 generally cannot work in non-agricultural jobs. Ages 14-15 face strict hour limits during school.

twist.text: Child labor violations are rising—DOL found 5,800 children illegally employed in 2022, a 283% increase since 2015.

climax.text: Some states are loosening protections, allowing younger workers and longer hours—reopening debates thought settled a century ago.

punchline.text: Child labor laws were won with blood. Weakening them forgets the cost.

quiz.question: What recent trend has been observed in child labor violations?
